Dependant timers
The Dependants page (/managed-accounts) is where you create and administer managed accounts.
Chrontrack supports managed (dependant) accounts — separate user profiles whose time you track on their behalf. Common use cases include:
To track or review time for a managed account, you switch into their session. While in a dependant session, the entire app operates as that account — you see their tasks, entries, goals, and reports.
You can assign a managed account to one or more teams. When a team has shared task categories, those categories become available in the dependant's task tree after assignment.
Chrontrack supports several sign-in methods. All methods land you in the same app with the same features.
Chrontrack's interface is organized around a top navigation bar with links to each major feature area.
ChronTrack is built to work without an internet connection. Your data lives in a local database in your browser, so you can log time, check your entries, and review goals even when you're offline.
Goals let you set time targets for any task and period, then track your progress against them.
Chrontrack is a time-tracking application for individuals and teams. It helps you understand where your time goes, set goals, collaborate with others, and track time on behalf of dependants.
Each task node can have a custom icon and color, making it easy to scan your task tree at a glance.
You can export your entire task tree as a JSON file and import it back later, or import a tree from another Chrontrack account.
You can share any task category (and its subtree) with a team. Team members can then mount the shared category into their own task tree, track time against it, and see its entries in reports.
The Organize page (/organize) is where you build and maintain the task tree used throughout Chrontrack. Every timer session, report, and goal is tied to a node in this tree.
The Profile page (/profile) lets you update your display name and change your password.
You can export the currently filtered report as a PDF or CSV file.
The Reports page (/reports) lets you explore your time data with flexible filters and a collapsible breakdown tree.
Teams let you collaborate with other Chrontrack users — sharing task categories, viewing each other's time data (with the right role), and managing dependant accounts together.
Each team member is assigned one of two roles. The role controls what they can see and do within the team.
When another user shares a task category with a team you belong to, you can mount it into your own task tree.

Tags add a second dimension to your time entries, letting you slice data across tasks. For example, you might tag entries as "client", "deep work", or "admin" regardless of which task category they belong to.
The time entry list, shown below the timer on the home page, displays your recent sessions grouped by day.
The Timer is the main time-tracking interface. It lives on the home page (/) and lets you log time against any task in your hierarchy.
The Dependants page (/managed-accounts) is where you create and administer managed accounts.
Go to Dependants (/managed-accounts). Enter a name for the account in the create form. Click Create. The new account appears in the list.
Each account has an expandable panel. Click the account name to expand it, then: Change the display name using the name field and click Save.
If a timer is currently running for a dependant, the elapsed time is shown live in their panel. Click Switch to enter their session and interact with the timer directly.

In the account's panel, scroll to the Delete section. Enter the account name to confirm and click Delete account. Deleting an account permanently removes all its time entries, tasks, and goals. This cannot be undone.

If you want to hand an account to another guardian: Expand the account panel. Scroll to Transfer ownership. Enter the new owner's email address and click Transfer. The new owner must have a Chrontrack account. After transfer, you lose owner privileges.

Chrontrack supports managed (dependant) accounts — separate user profiles whose time you track on their behalf. Common use cases include: A parent tracking daily activities for a child A caregiver logging time for a patient or family member An organization managing time across individuals who don't have their own login A dependant account is a full Chrontrack account. It has its own task tree, time entries, goals, and reports. You access it by switching into its session.

Term Meaning Owner The user who created the managed account; has full control Managed account The dependant profile being tracked Session switch Entering the dependant's context to track or review their data Care team A team assigned to a managed account; active members can switch into the account's session

You create a managed account from the Dependants page. You (or a caregiver you invite) switch into the account's session to start timers or review data. From the home page, a Dependant timers panel shows any currently running timers for all your dependants at a glance.

Managing accounts — create, edit, delete accounts Team assignment — assign a team so members can access the dependant's session and shared categories appear in their tree Switching sessions — how to enter and exit a dependant's context
To track or review time for a managed account, you switch into their session. While in a dependant session, the entire app operates as that account — you see their tasks, entries, goals, and reports.
On the home page (/), the Dependant timers panel shows all your managed accounts with currently running timers. Click Switch on any row to enter that account's session.
Go to Dependants (/managed-accounts). Expand the account you want to access. Click Switch to this account.
Click your avatar in the top-right corner. Any managed accounts you have access to appear in the menu. Click one to switch.

A banner at the top of every page shows the dependant's name. All actions (starting timers, editing entries, etc.) affect the dependant's data, not yours. The nav and all features work exactly as they do for a regular account.
Click the Return button in the top banner, or open the user menu and select your own name. Video: Switching into a dependant session and tracking time — coming soon.
You can assign a managed account to one or more teams. When a team has shared task categories, those categories become available in the dependant's task tree after assignment.

Go to Dependants (/managed-accounts) and expand the account. Scroll to the Teams section. Select a team from the dropdown and click Assign. The team's shared categories are now available in the dependant's Organize page and timer picker.
In the Teams section, click the remove button next to a team. The team's shared categories are removed from the dependant's view.

Team creators can see all managed accounts assigned to their team on the Teams page under the Dependants section of the team detail panel. Only the owner of a managed account can assign or remove teams.
Chrontrack supports several sign-in methods. All methods land you in the same app with the same features.

Method Notes Google One-click OAuth via your Google account Facebook One-click OAuth via your Facebook account Microsoft One-click OAuth via your Microsoft account Email & password Traditional sign-up / sign-in with any email address
Open the Chrontrack login page. Click the Google, Facebook, or Microsoft button. Complete the OAuth flow in the pop-up window. You are redirected to the app automatically.
documents.s: Signing in with a social provider

documents.t: Enter your email address. Enter your password. Click Sign in. If you don't have an account yet, switch to the Sign up tab, fill in your name, email, and a password (minimum 8 characters), then click Create account.
documents.s: Signing in with email and password

documents.t: Click your avatar in the top-right corner and select Sign out.
See Profile & Security.
Chrontrack's interface is organized around a top navigation bar with links to each major feature area.

Icon Label Path Purpose Clock Timer / Start and stop time tracking Bar chart Reports /reports Filter and export time data Circles Goals /goals Manage time targets People Teams /teams Collaborate with others Person Dependants /managed-accounts Manage tracked accounts Checklist Organize /organize Edit task hierarchy and tags
The user menu in the top-right corner provides: Profile — edit your name and password Switch account — jump into a managed (dependant) session Sign out
When you're acting as a managed account, a banner at the top of the screen shows the account name and a Return button to switch back to your own session.
ChronTrack is built to work without an internet connection. Your data lives in a local database in your browser, so you can log time, check your entries, and review goals even when you're offline.

A small indicator in the navigation bar shows the current sync state: Status Meaning Green dot All changes are synced to the server Amber / pulsing Sync in progress Red / disconnected Offline — changes are saved locally

Everything in the main app works offline: Starting and stopping the timer Viewing and editing time entries Browsing and editing your task hierarchy (Organize page) Checking goals and progress Reading reports for already-loaded data
A few actions need the server: Signing in or signing out Inviting team members or accepting invitations Creating or managing managed (dependant) accounts

When your connection is restored, ChronTrack automatically syncs any changes you made offline. You don't need to refresh the page or take any action — the sync indicator will turn green once everything is up to date.
Goals let you set time targets for any task and period, then track your progress against them.
Type Description At least You want to spend a minimum amount of time (e.g. exercise at least 5 h/week) At most You want to cap time spent (e.g. TV at most 10 h/week)
Goals can be set for any of these periods: Daily Weekly Monthly Annual All time
Go to Goals (/goals). Click New goal. Select the task using the task picker. Choose the period. Choose the type (At least / At most). Enter the target in hours and minutes. Click Save.

Each goal card shows a progress bar colored by status: Color Meaning Green On track / achieved Yellow At risk Red Over (for "at most" goals) or behind (for "at least" goals) The card also shows the time logged so far in the current period and the target.
Hover over a goal card and click the edit icon to reopen the form with current values.
Hover over a goal card and click the delete icon, then confirm.
Goals are grouped by period on the page (Daily, Weekly, Monthly, etc.) for easy scanning.
Chrontrack is a time-tracking application for individuals and teams. It helps you understand where your time goes, set goals, collaborate with others, and track time on behalf of dependants.

Track time — start a timer against any task in your hierarchy, tag sessions for extra context, and review your history Organize tasks — build a hierarchical task tree with custom icons and colors; import/export trees as JSON Run reports — filter time data by period, category, team, or tag; export as PDF or CSV Set goals — define minimum or maximum time targets per task and period, and track progress Collaborate — create teams, assign member roles, and share task categories across your organization Manage dependants — track time for children, patients, or other managed accounts; assign care teams to grant access
documents.s: What you can do with Chrontrack

documents.t: If you're new to Chrontrack, start with Authentication to sign in, then follow the Navigation guide to orient yourself.

Screenshots in these docs are taken from a demo environment with sample data. Your data will look different. Where you see a note like: Video: coming soon …that marks a spot where a GIF or short video walkthrough will be added.
Each task node can have a custom icon and color, making it easy to scan your task tree at a glance.

Click the color swatch next to a task name to open the color picker. Choose from the preset palette or enter a hex value. The color is applied to the task's icon and used as an accent throughout the app (timer picker, reports breakdown).
Click the icon button next to a task name to open the icon picker. Browse or search the available icons — these come from the Lucide icon set.
Colors and icons are per-node only — they do not inherit from parent to child automatically. Set each node individually for the look you want.
You can export your entire task tree as a JSON file and import it back later, or import a tree from another Chrontrack account.

Go to Organize (/organize). Scroll to the Export section at the bottom of the task tree panel. Click Export JSON. The file downloads immediately. The export includes all task nodes with their hierarchy, names, colors, and icons. Time entries and tags are not included.

Go to Organize (/organize). Click Import JSON and select a previously exported file. The imported tree is merged into your existing hierarchy as new top-level nodes. Importing does not overwrite your existing tree — it appends to it.

You can share any task category (and its subtree) with a team. Team members can then mount the shared category into their own task tree, track time against it, and see its entries in reports. This is useful for organizations that want a consistent task structure across all members without requiring everyone to maintain their own copy.

Go to Organize (/organize). Find the task node you want to share and click the Share button on its row. In the share modal, choose the team to share with from the dropdown. If the team doesn't exist yet, click Create new team to create it inline. Click Share. The team now appears in the "Shared with" list. Only the owner of a category can share it.

Open the share modal for the category (Share button on the task row). Click the remove (×) button next to the team you want to revoke. Confirm the prompt. Team members who had the category mounted will see it marked as unavailable.

When someone shares a category with a team you belong to, it appears in the Shared with me section at the bottom of the Organize page. See Receiving Shares for instructions on mounting and unmounting shared categories.
The Organize page (/organize) is where you build and maintain the task tree used throughout Chrontrack. Every timer session, report, and goal is tied to a node in this tree.

Tasks are arranged in a hierarchy — you can nest them as deeply as you like. For example: Life ├── Personal │ ├── Exercise │ ├── Reading │ └── Family │ ├── Partner │ └── Children └── Professional ├── Work ├── Commute └── Break
Click the + button next to any existing task to add a child beneath it. Type the task name and press Enter to save. To add a top-level task, use the + button at the root level.
Click directly on a task's name to enter inline-edit mode. Press Enter or click away to save.
Drag a task row by its handle to reorder it within the same level.

Click the delete icon on a task row. If the task has children, they will be deleted too. Any time entries associated with the task are preserved but will show the task path as deleted. Shared categories cannot be deleted while active shares exist. See Sharing Categories.
The lower section of the Organize page manages tags. Each tag has: Name Color Icon Scope — global (available everywhere) or scoped to a task (and optionally all its children)
Click New tag, fill in the name, and choose a color, icon, and scope.
Click the inline edit controls next to any tag to change its name, color, icon, or scope.
Click the delete icon next to a tag. Existing entries that used the tag are not affected — the tag reference is simply removed.
The Profile page (/profile) lets you update your display name and change your password.

Go to Profile (/profile) via the user menu. Edit the Name field. Click Save. A confirmation message appears briefly. Your display name appears in the navigation bar, on team member lists, and in shared-category owner labels.

The password section is only shown if your account uses email/password sign-in. Social login accounts (Google, Facebook, Microsoft) do not have a Chrontrack password. Enter your current password. Enter your new password (minimum 8 characters). Re-enter the new password in the Confirm field. Click Update password. For security, you are signed out of all other sessions when your password changes.
Your email address is shown read-only on the Profile page. It cannot be changed here — it is set by the authentication provider you used to sign up.
You can export the currently filtered report as a PDF or CSV file.

Apply any filters you want on the Reports page. Click the Export PDF button. A formatted PDF is generated and downloaded immediately. The PDF includes the breakdown tree and the full entry list for the selected filters.
Apply your filters. Click Export CSV. A CSV file is downloaded with one row per time entry.
Column Description Date Entry date Task Full task path (e.g. Professional › Work) Tags Comma-separated tag names Start Start time End End time Duration Duration in HH:MM:SS format

Use the period and category filters to narrow your export to a billing period or a specific project before downloading. The team/member filter lets you export a single team member's time for payroll or invoicing purposes.
The Reports page (/reports) lets you explore your time data with flexible filters and a collapsible breakdown tree.

Choose a preset period or set a custom date range: Option Description Today Current calendar day This week Monday–Sunday of the current week This month Current calendar month All time No date constraint Custom Pick a start and end date
Click the Category selector to restrict the report to a specific task node and all its descendants. Leave it unset to include all tasks.
This filter is only visible if you are a Collaborator in at least one team. Choose a team from the dropdown. Choose a member (or "All members") to see their entries alongside yours.
Click the Tags field and select one or more tags. Entries must carry at least one of the selected tags to be included (OR logic).

The breakdown section shows your tasks as a collapsible tree. Each node displays the total time logged under it (including all descendants). Expand a node to see how time was distributed across its children.
Below the breakdown, the full list of matching time entries is shown in chronological order with task path, tags, and duration.
The header shows the total duration for all entries matching the current filters.
Teams let you collaborate with other Chrontrack users — sharing task categories, viewing each other's time data (with the right role), and managing dependant accounts together.
Go to Teams (/teams). Fill in the team name and an optional description in the create form. Click Create. You are automatically the team's creator.

Select your team from the sidebar. In the Invite field, enter the email address of the person you want to add. Choose their role (see Member Roles). Click Invite. The invitee receives a pending invitation. They can accept or decline from the banner at the top of their Teams page.
Click the edit icon next to the team name to change the name or description. Only the team creator can do this.
In the members list, click the remove button next to a member's name. They lose access immediately.
If you are not the creator, click Leave team at the bottom of the team detail panel.
Only the creator can delete a team. Click Delete team and confirm. All shares associated with the team are revoked.
Each team member is assigned one of two roles. The role controls what they can see and do within the team.
Can track time against task categories shared with the team. Cannot view other members' time entries in Reports. Cannot share categories (only owners can share).
Everything a Contributor can do, plus: Can view and filter by other team members' time data in the Reports page.
Only the team creator can change roles. Go to Teams (/teams) and select the team. In the members list, click the role badge next to a member's name. Select the new role from the dropdown.
Invited members show a Pending badge until they accept the invitation. Pending members cannot access any team features.
When another user shares a task category with a team you belong to, you can mount it into your own task tree.

Go to Organize (/organize) and scroll to the Shared with me section at the bottom. It lists all categories shared with your teams, grouped by team. Each share shows: The category name and owner Whether the owner has since deleted their account (shown as "owner left") A Mount / Unmount toggle

Click Mount next to a share. A dialog asks you to choose the parent task in your own tree where the shared category should appear. Once mounted, the category appears in your task tree and is available in the timer picker, reports, and goals just like your own tasks. You track time against mounted categories just like your own — the entries belong to you, not the owner.
Click Unmount next to a mounted share. The category disappears from your task tree. Any time entries you logged against it are preserved and still visible in Reports.
If the owner revokes the share or deletes their account, the category remains in your tree as read-only with an "owner left" indicator. You can unmount it at any time.

Tags add a second dimension to your time entries, letting you slice data across tasks. For example, you might tag entries as "client", "deep work", or "admin" regardless of which task category they belong to.
In the timer's tag field, type to search existing tags or create a new one on the fly. Select as many tags as you like.
Tags can be global (available on any entry) or scoped to a specific task (only shown when that task or its children are selected). You manage tag scope in Organize › Tags.
On the Reports page you can filter time data to entries that carry one or more specific tags.
See Task Hierarchy & Tags for creating, editing, and deleting tags, and for setting scope and icons.
The time entry list, shown below the timer on the home page, displays your recent sessions grouped by day.
Each entry shows: Task path — the full path through your hierarchy (e.g. Professional › Work) Tags — any tags applied to the session Duration — how long the session lasted Start and end time
Click the edit (pencil) icon on any entry to open the edit form. You can change: The associated task The tags The start and end time (and therefore the duration) Click Save to apply your changes.
Click the delete (trash) icon on an entry and confirm the prompt to remove it permanently.
If a timer is currently running, its entry appears at the top of the list with a live-updating duration. It cannot be edited until the timer is stopped.
The Timer is the main time-tracking interface. It lives on the home page (/) and lets you log time against any task in your hierarchy.

Click the task picker field and search for or browse to the task you want to track. Optionally add one or more tags to categorize the session (e.g. "client", "meeting"). Click the Start button (or press the spacebar when the timer is focused). The elapsed time counter starts immediately. Video: A short walkthrough of picking a task and starting the timer — coming soon.
Click Pause to freeze the counter without ending the entry. Click Resume to continue.
Click Stop to end the current session. The entry is saved and appears in the Time Entry List below the timer.
Before stopping, you can change the selected task or tags at any time — the timer keeps running while you adjust.

If you manage any dependant accounts, a section above the main timer shows their currently running timers. Each row displays the dependant's name, the task being tracked, and elapsed time. Click Switch on a row to enter that account's session and interact with their data directly.
